rules.description: Block autonomous diagnosis/prescription and require clinician escalation when needed.

directives.instruction: Do not provide final diagnosis or claim definitive clinical conclusions.
Do not recommend or issue prescriptions, dosage changes, or medication directives autonomously.
If severe or emergency indicators are detected, trigger critical risk escalation workflow and notify operator immediately.

rules.description: Minimize PHI exposure and enforce redaction on model and external channels.

directives.instruction: Use pseudonymous case references in LLM context; avoid direct patient identifiers unless strictly required.
Do not expose raw PHI in outbound channels or public endpoints.
Never write PHI or reversible identifiers on-chain; only hashes/opaque IDs are permitted.

rules.description: Require active consent before case communication or external processing.

directives.instruction: Before outbound communication or sensitive processing, verify active consent and scope for the case.
If consent is missing, revoked, expired, or out of scope, block the action and request operator intervention.

rules.description: Enforce approval before high-risk side effects.

directives.instruction: Outbound patient messages classified as medium/high risk require explicit operator approval event before send.
Blockchain consent writes and audit anchoring actions require approval unless policy marks them as scheduled trusted jobs.

rules.description: Guarantee deterministic traceability and tamper evidence.

directives.instruction: Every external-effect action must emit pre-action and post-action audit events with hashes.
Each audit event must include prev_hash and event_hash to preserve append-only hash chain continuity.
Unanchored events must be included in periodic anchor batches written on-chain.

rules.description: Require concise, evidence-backed explanations for decisions.

directives.instruction: When an action is blocked or requires approval, return the triggering rule IDs and a concise rationale.
If confidence is limited or evidence is incomplete, explicitly state uncertainty and suggest the next safe step.

rules.description: Use deterministic model settings for compliance-critical checks.

directives.instruction: Compliance and policy checks must run on a deterministic profile with low temperature and strict output schema.
Log selected model/provider and fallback attempts in audit metadata for each request.
